maturity_justification: Two specimens = comparison, not pattern. All observations start DESCRIPTIVE per convergence maturity discipline.

netbox.label: Full governed semantic evolution specimen
netbox.evidence_channel: Code graph (1,494 AST authority edges, 12 CEUs)

blueedge.label: Post-genesis governed semantic replay specimen
blueedge.corridor_boundary: Operates on already-materialized semantic substrates. Does NOT prove raw intake genesis.

observations.title: Governance friction strengthens substrate
observations.description: In both specimens, governance friction (operator challenge, contestation, rejection) directly triggered substrate strengthening that improved corpus quality. The friction was not noise — it was signal that drove enrichment.

netbox_evidence.friction_events: 2 contested (AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY, HERO_MOMENT_GROUNDING) → 2 arbitrated → both accepted with enrichment
netbox_evidence.enrichment_response: 3-stream substrate strengthening: authority enrichment feasibility → authority enrichment pipeline → hero moment reconciliation
netbox_evidence.corpus_impact: DERIVED→DIRECT_EVIDENCE on 10 AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY propositions, 5/6 HERO_MOMENT reconciled to ALIGNED, mean confidence 0.872→0.897

blueedge_evidence.friction_events: 9 contested → 9 arbitrated → 9 accepted. 14 rejected outright. 5 governance findings surfaced.
blueedge_evidence.enrichment_response: RC-04 evidence enrichment: domain ID correction via semantic name matching (addressing GF-RC03-001), 31 enrichment events
blueedge_evidence.corpus_impact: 12 domain propositions corrected, 15 capability domain refs corrected, mean confidence 0.728→0.741. Debt evolution: 4 improved, 6 worsened (honest), 0 resolved.

observations.divergence: NetBox enrichment used code graph authority (1,494 AST edges) producing tier conversion (DERIVED→DIRECT_EVIDENCE). BlueEdge enrichment used semantic name matching correcting evidence linkage without tier conversion. Different mechanisms, same pattern: friction → enrichment → improved corpus.

observations.title: Semantic propositions require operator authority
observations.description: In both specimens, AI-derived semantic propositions required explicit human operator review before qualification could advance. The non-automatable boundary held — no system actor could self-authorize progression.

netbox_evidence.review_obligations: 6 obligations, all resolved through governed operator review
netbox_evidence.non_automatable_boundary: 7 boundaries enforced — no system actor promotion, elevation, resolution, acceptance, closure, advancement, or override

blueedge_evidence.review_actions: 62 direct accept, 9 contested → arbitrated → accepted, 14 rejected
blueedge_evidence.non_automatable_boundary: Same 7 boundaries enforced. System actors rejected at boundary.

observations.divergence: NetBox reviewed 6 class-level obligations (one per proposition class). BlueEdge reviewed 85 individual propositions. Different granularity, same authority requirement: human operator must act.

observations.title: Deterministic revalidation is necessary before qualification trust
observations.description: In both specimens, deterministic multi-phase revalidation was required after enrichment and before qualification advancement. The revalidation framework transferred across specimen types without modification of the validation contract structure.

netbox_evidence.phases: Schema integrity, spine consistency, AUTHORITY_TOPOLOGY enrichment, HERO_MOMENT_GROUNDING, corpus quality, reconciliation metrics, cross-class consistency, SQO governance, corpus summary, enrichment lineage

blueedge_evidence.phases: Structural integrity, evidence integrity, confidence realism, governance integrity, enrichment integrity, checkpoint integrity, spine integrity, SQO state consistency, corpus evolution

observations.divergence: NetBox's code graph phases (centrality, coupling, topology checks) were replaced in BlueEdge with evidence integrity and enrichment integrity phases appropriate for document-derived evidence. Same structural rigor, adapted for different evidence channels.

observations.title: Weak claims must remain rejected or confidence-limited
observations.description: In both specimens, the governance lifecycle preserved rejection and confidence limitation as valid terminal states. Neither specimen forced weak claims to acceptance to satisfy coverage targets.

netbox_evidence.rejected: CLUSTER_ARCHITECTURE (SP-netbox-0075) — permanently rejected. 0.604 confidence, DERIVED tier, NOVEL reconciliation. Singleton cross-cutting cluster claim from single observation. Preserved in lineage, not deleted.
netbox_evidence.limited: 8 NOVEL propositions retained at DERIVED tier — not forced to ALIGNED

blueedge_evidence.rejected: 14 propositions rejected — SDC parsing artifacts (3), SDC domain overflow (2), functional overlap misclassification (4), domain ID mismatch (3), grounding status conflict (2). All preserved in lineage.
blueedge_evidence.limited: 3 NO_SDC_MATCH domains capped at 0.50 confidence. WEAKLY_GROUNDED propositions penalized. 15 debt items: 0 resolved, all requiring L5 authority PATH B cannot provide.
blueedge_evidence.mean_confidence: 0.741 — honest, reflecting PATH B evidence limitations

observations.divergence: NetBox rejected 1/77 (1.3%) with all blockers resolved. BlueEdge rejected 14/85 (16.5%) with 0/15 blockers resolved. BlueEdge's higher rejection rate reflects genuine PATH B evidence limitations — the system is more honest about what document-derived evidence can prove.

observations.title: Lineage and proof objects make cognition auditable
observations.description: In both specimens, the governed lifecycle produced a complete audit trail through spine objects, governance events, checkpoint freezes, and proof capsules. Cognition is reconstructable from artifacts alone — no session memory required.

netbox_evidence.replay_validation: 12/12 PASS — governance state fully reconstructable from event logs, derivation lineage, and spine objects
netbox_evidence.learning_events: 14 PROPOSED (5 governance loop, 3 SPE derivation, 6 substrate enrichment)
netbox_evidence.session_independence: Explicitly verified: no state requires Claude-session memory to reconstruct

blueedge_evidence.spine_objects: 9 accumulated (evidence, projection, proposition, qualification transition, replay corridor)
blueedge_evidence.enrichment_log: 31 events with pre/post values for every correction

observations.divergence: NetBox used replay validation (12 reconstruction tests). BlueEdge used checkpoint-based chronicle architecture (8 frozen snapshots with semantic phase markers). Different auditability mechanisms, same outcome: full cognitive reconstructability.

observations.title: S2 means governed semantic lifecycle completion, not semantic perfection
observations.description: In both specimens, S2 was earned by completing the governed semantic lifecycle (derivation, review, enrichment, revalidation) — not by achieving perfect semantic coverage or resolving all debt. The qualification recognizes governance discipline, not ontological completeness.

netbox_evidence.s2_state: S2 granted with 1 rejected proposition (CLUSTER_ARCHITECTURE preserved), 8 NOVEL/DERIVED propositions retained
netbox_evidence.debt_state: Qualification debt COMPLETE but CLUSTER_ARCHITECTURE rejection is permanent record

blueedge_evidence.blockers: 15 active — all block S3 requiring L5 authority. 0 resolved.
blueedge_evidence.governance_lifecycle: 6 streams, 94 events, governed lifecycle proven on post-genesis substrates

observations.divergence: NetBox achieved S2 with all blockers resolved (full PATH A structural authority). BlueEdge achieved S2_GOVERNED with 0 blockers resolved (PATH B cannot provide L5 authority). Both are valid S2 — the difference is in the evidence ceiling, not in the governance discipline. NetBox's S2 is advancement-ready. BlueEdge's S2 is governance-proven but structurally bounded.
observations.boundary_note: BlueEdge's S2_GOVERNED proves governed lifecycle completion within a post-genesis replay corridor. It does NOT prove full onboarding-origin genesis. The two S2 qualifications are operationally equivalent in governance discipline but structurally different in evidence depth.

observations.title: Different evidence corridors enter the same governed spine model
observations.description: Two fundamentally different evidence channels — code graph structural analysis (PATH A) and HTML document semantic derivation (PATH B) — both produced propositions, governance events, and spine objects that entered the same governed spine model and underwent the same revalidation framework.

netbox_evidence.evidence_channel: PATH A: GitHub repository code graph
netbox_evidence.evidence_depth: 2,540 nodes, 3,614 IMPORTS, 1,089 ranked files, 1,494 AST authority edges
netbox_evidence.enrichment_mechanism: AST authority extraction — code-structural enrichment producing tier conversion

blueedge_evidence.evidence_depth: 17 CSR domains, 42 capabilities, 89 components, 28 vault claims
blueedge_evidence.enrichment_mechanism: Semantic name matching — evidence linkage correction without tier conversion

observations.divergence: Completely different proposition classes (code-structural vs document-semantic), different enrichment mechanisms (AST edges vs name matching), different evidence depths (1,494 AST edges vs 505KB HTML). Yet both produce spine objects in the same governed model and pass through the same 9-phase/48-check revalidation framework.
observations.significance: This is the strongest convergence evidence: the governed spine model is evidence-channel-independent. The governance framework does not care WHERE the evidence came from — it cares that the evidence was governed.

dimensions.convergence: DIVERGENT — PATH B has more evidence limitations
DIVERGENT — code graph produces higher confidence than document parsing
DIVERGENT — PATH A achieves higher structural grounding

dimensions.netbox: AST authority edges (code graph structural enrichment)
dimensions.convergence: DIVERGENT — different evidence sources, same enrichment intent
CONVERGENT in pattern — enrichment improves corpus quality
CONVERGENT — identical framework structure and result
DIVERGENT — PATH B cannot provide L5 authority
CONVERGENT in governance discipline, DIVERGENT in evidence depth

dimensions.blueedge: 94 governance events, 8 checkpoints, 9 spine objects, full chronicle
dimensions.convergence: CONVERGENT — both fully auditable from artifacts alone

law_assessment.methodology: Two specimens = comparison, not pattern. Observations that are SUPPORTED_BY_BOTH_SPECIMENS are candidate governed cognition laws — they hold across both specimens but require additional specimens to confirm as laws. No observation is promoted to LAW status with only two specimens.

supported_laws.statement: Semantic propositions require operator authority — the non-automatable boundary holds across specimen types
supported_laws.promotion_criteria: Third specimen with operator review exercising same authority model

supported_laws.statement: Deterministic revalidation is necessary before qualification trust
supported_laws.promotion_criteria: Third specimen passing adapted revalidation framework

supported_laws.statement: Weak claims must remain rejected or confidence-limited — the system does not inflate
supported_laws.promotion_criteria: Third specimen demonstrating rejection/limitation without coverage pressure

supported_laws.statement: Lineage and proof objects make cognition auditable — session-independent reconstructability
supported_laws.promotion_criteria: Third specimen achieving auditability through governed artifacts

supported_laws.statement: S2 means governed semantic lifecycle completion, not semantic perfection
supported_laws.promotion_criteria: Third specimen qualifying at S2 with honest limitations documented

supported_laws.statement: The governed spine model is evidence-channel-independent
supported_laws.promotion_criteria: Third evidence channel (e.g., API documentation, design artifacts) entering same spine model

law_assessment.note: With only two specimens, ALL laws are candidates regardless of bilateral evidence strength. The distinction between 'supported' and 'candidate' here reflects evidence quality within the comparison, not pattern maturity. True LAW status requires 3+ specimens per convergence maturity discipline.

scope_boundary.what_this_proves: Two independent specimens demonstrate convergent governance patterns — the governance lifecycle is not a one-specimen trick.
what_this_does_not_prove: BlueEdge does NOT prove raw intake genesis — it proves governed replay qualification over a post-genesis semantic corridor
These observations are DESCRIPTIVE, not LAWS — two specimens is comparison, not pattern
Convergence in governance discipline does NOT imply convergence in evidence depth — PATH A and PATH B have fundamentally different ceilings
No claims about marketplace, modules, agentic AI, or Cortex architecture
